QF.id: XIII Sales Practices, Code of Conduct and Investor Protection_7

question.text: What is the purpose of the Investor Protection Fund (IPF) maintained by stock exchanges?

answer.text: IPF compensates investors for losses caused by the default of a trading member — when a defaulter's assets are insufficient to meet all claims, IPF provides additional protection
IPF is a fund that guarantees profits to all derivative market investors
IPF pays interest on margin deposits held by the clearing corporation
IPF is used by the exchange to fund new product development in derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Each exchange maintains an IPF to protect investors against trading member defaults. If a broker defaults and their assets are insufficient to settle all client claims, the IPF covers the shortfall up to specified limits. The fund is built through contributions from penalties, exchange earnings, and interest income. **The Trap:** IPF doesn't guarantee profits or pay interest on margins. It only comes into play when a trading member defaults AND their own assets are insufficient. **Cross-Exam Context:** IPF is maintained across all exchange segments including equity, currency, and interest rate derivatives. Series I, IV, and VIII all reference it. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** IPF protection has limits — very large claims may not be fully covered. This is why choosing a well-capitalized broker is important for investor protection.
